page: Microsoft Azure F8ams_v6 is a memory-intensive virtual machine belonging to the Famsv6 server family. Powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs operating at a base frequency of 3.7 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, but it is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. The hypervisor layer is managed via Microsoft Hyper-V.

Benchmark data highlights strong single-core capabilities. The instance achieves top-tier status in stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench text processing. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench single-core scores also rank in the top 25%. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core metrics, falls within the average range. A notable performance tradeoff is observed in memory bandwidth; while small-block cached reads and large-block writes perform at average levels, uncached large-block read bandwidth is relatively weak, placing in the bottom 25%.

Given its high memory density of 8 GB per vCPU and dedicated AMD EPYC compute resources, the F8ams_v6 offers a balanced cost-to-resource profile for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive multi-threaded scaling. The absence of bundled local storage means storage costs are decoupled, allowing users to provision Premium Storage as needed. This instance is well-suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, text processing pipelines, single-threaded data decompression, and moderate-scale web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure F8ams_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the Famsv6 family, designed for memory-intensive workloads. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. While the instance lacks local storage and GPUs, it supports Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU execution and text processing, alongside strong single-threaded decompression and small-model LLM text generation. However, uncached large-block memory read bandwidth represents a performance tradeoff, scoring in the bottom 25%. This server is optimized for database operations, text processing, and memory-heavy applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F8ams_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and top-tier single-core performance, making it highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F8ams_v6 is a memory-intensive virtual machine with 8 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and strong single-core benchmark performance.
tagline: A memory-intensive virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and high memory density for single-core and database workloads.
